parentQuestion: A structure that is composed of two or more tissue types that work together to perform specific functions for the body is a(n) ___.

scaffolds.q: The body is organized in levels of increasing complexity.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! From atoms all the way up to the complete organism.

scaffolds.q: CELLS are the basic units of life.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Cells are the smallest living units that can function independently.

scaffolds.q: A TISSUE is a group of similar cells working together.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Muscle tissue = muscle cells. Nervous tissue = nerve cells. Similar cells, common function.

scaffolds.q: There are FOUR primary tissue types: epithelial, connective, muscle, nervous.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! These four tissue types make up all the organs in your body.

scaffolds.q: An ORGAN is made of TWO OR MORE tissue types working together.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The heart has muscle tissue, connective tissue, epithelial tissue, and nerve tissue.

scaffolds.q: An ORGAN SYSTEM is a group of ORGANS working together.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The cardiovascular system includes heart, blood vessels, and blood.

scaffolds.q: The hierarchy: cells → tissues → ORGANS → organ systems → organism.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Organs are made of multiple tissues, and are part of organ systems.

scaffolds.q: Multiple tissue types working together = an ORGAN.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! That's the definition of an organ - multiple tissues, specific function.

scaffolds.q: The answer is ORGAN - composed of 2+ tissue types for a specific function.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not a tissue (single cell type), not an organ system (multiple organs).
